originalTitle: Invest All $350K In The S&P 500 Or Dollar Cost Average? Dave Ramsey Says One Thing Is Certain, Trump Is 'Going To Do A Trump Thing'
summary: Putting hundreds of thousands of dollars into the stock market all at once can feel risky, especially when political headlines, international conflicts and unexpected events can send stocks sharply higher or lower. That’s why personal finance expert Dave Ramsey told...
content:
Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

Putting hundreds of thousands of dollars into the stock market all at once can feel risky, especially when political headlines, international conflicts and unexpected events can send stocks sharply higher or lower.

That's why personal finance expert Dave Ramsey told a " The Ramsey Show " listener considering a $350,000 investment that market volatility is inevitable because President Donald Trump is "going to do a Trump thing" at some point. The real question, Ramsey said, is whether investors can stay calm when it happens.

Find out if your retirement plan is exposed to risks most investors overlook — get matched with a fiduciary adviser today.

Still on the fence? Coverage gets harder to lock in as you age. Ladder lets eligible applicants apply for term life insurance in minutes — no medical exam required up to $3 million, while you're still eligible.

The Math Favors A Lump-Sum Investment?

Gabriel wrote into the show saying that he and his family recently sold their home. Since they don't expect to buy another property for four or five years, they were considering putting the money into the S&P 500 and other index funds. The question was whether to invest the entire amount at once or spread it out gradually through dollar-cost averaging.

"If you understand what you're putting money into and you've looked at the track record of it, the S&P 500, the math would tell you to put it all in at once," he said.

He explained that money invested immediately has more time to benefit from market growth. Using a hypothetical example, Ramsey said a $100,000 investment made at the beginning of a year in which the market gains 25% would grow to roughly $125,000. Someone investing that same amount gradually over 12 months would end up with significantly less because much of the money would spend part of the year sitting in cash.

Still, Ramsey acknowledged that investing everything at once can be emotionally difficult.

"The only reason a dollar cost average is if you're emotionally not committed to this process," he said, "and the first time Trump decides to bomb Iran and the market goes down, you freak out."

Trending: More than 1.5 million people are already quietly investing through Stash — start with as little as $1, earn stock on everyday purchases, and get a 3% IRA match with Stash+.

Ramsey warned that investors should expect periods of turbulence.

"He's going to do a Trump thing. You can count on it," Ramsey said. "I don't know what the next one's going to be, but there's going to be a Trump thing. Sometimes it causes the market to go up . Sometimes it causes the market to go down ."

He added that countless other events can move markets as well, from geopolitical conflicts to economic news and unexpected global developments.

Throughout the conversation, Ramsey pointed to examples of markets recovering after major declines . He discussed the sharp selloff during the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic, when stocks plunged as uncertainty spread across the economy.

"The market recovers common sense over time," Ramsey said.

He also referenced recent market reactions to tensions involving Iran, adding that some of the biggest headline-driven drops often recover faster than investors expect.

See Also: Most budgeting apps ignore your investments. Empower doesn't — it syncs your 401(k), IRA, bank, and credit accounts into one real-time dashboard.

Ramsey's broader message was that long-term investors usually get into trouble when they panic and sell during downturns. "No one gets hurt on a roller coaster except those that get off in the middle of the ride," he said. "So, if you think you're going to jump, dollar cost average because it keeps you from freaking out quite so much."

Not everyone can get a personal investing question answered by Ramsey. For people trying to decide how to invest a large sum, prepare for retirement or build a long-term financial strategy, AdviserMatch can connect them with financial advisors who offer investing and financial planning guidance. Consultations are no-obligation, and it takes only about two minutes to get started.

In Gabriel's case, if he can handle the market's ups and downs without panicking, investing the full $350,000 immediately offers the strongest long-term mathematical case, according to Ramsey.

Building Wealth Across More Than Just the Market

Building a resilient portfolio means thinking beyond a single asset or market trend. Economic cycles shift, sectors rise and fall, and no one investment performs well in every environment. That's why many investors look to diversify with platforms that provide access to real estate, fixed-income opportunities, precious metals, and even self-directed retirement accounts. By spreading exposure across multiple asset classes, it becomes easier to manage risk, capture steady returns, and create long-term wealth that isn't tied to the fortunes of just one company or industry.

Backed by Jeff Bezos, Arrived Homes makes real estate investing accessible with a low barrier to entry. Investors can buy fractional shares of single-family rentals and vacation homes starting with as little as $100 . This allows everyday investors to diversify into real estate, collect rental income, and build long-term wealth without needing to manage properties directly.

Residential real estate has historically provided investors with income potential and long-term appreciation, but direct ownership can be expensive and time-consuming. ARK7 enables investors to buy fractional shares of rental properties, offering access to potential rental income and real estate exposure without property management responsibilities. By lowering the barrier to entry, the platform gives investors another way to diversify
chineseTitle: 将 35 万美元全部投资于标准普尔 500 指数还是平均成本指数？戴夫·拉姆齐表示有一件事是确定的，特朗普“会做特朗普的事”
chineseSummary: 一次性将数十万美元投入股市可能会让人感觉有风险，尤其是当政治头条新闻、国际冲突和意外事件可能导致股市大幅上涨或下跌时。这就是为什么个人理财专家戴夫拉姆齐告诉......
chineseContent:
Benzinga 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可能通过以下链接赚取某些项目的佣金或收入。

一次性将数十万美元投入股市可能会让人感觉有风险，尤其是当政治头条新闻、国际冲突和意外事件可能导致股市大幅上涨或下跌时。

这就是为什么个人理财专家戴夫·拉姆齐 (Dave Ramsey) 在考虑 35 万美元的投资时告诉“拉姆齐秀”听众，市场波动是不可避免的，因为唐纳德·特朗普总统在某个时候“会做特朗普的事情”。拉姆齐说，真正的问题是投资者在这种情况发生时能否保持冷静。

了解您的退休计划是否面临大多数投资者忽视的风险——立即与信托顾问进行匹配。

还在围墙吗？随着年龄的增长，保险范围变得越来越难锁定。 Ladder 可让符合资格的申请人在几分钟内申请定期人寿保险 - 无需进行体检，金额高达 300 万美元，而您仍然符合资格。

数学有利于一次性投资吗？

加布里埃尔在节目中写道，他和他的家人最近卖掉了他们的房子。由于他们预计四五年内不会购买另一处房产，因此他们正在考虑将资金投入标准普尔 500 指数和其他指数基金。问题是是一次性投入全部资金，还是通过平均成本法逐步分散投资。

他说：“如果你了解你要投入的资金是什么，并且你看过它的业绩记录，即标准普尔 500 指数，那么数学会告诉你立即投入所有资金。”

他解释说，立即投入的资金有更多时间从市场增长中受益。拉姆齐举了一个假设的例子，表示年初投资 10 万美元，如果市场上涨 25%，则投资额将增至大约 12.5 万美元。如果有人在 12 个月内逐渐投资相同金额，最终的收益将显着减少，因为大部分资金将在一年中的部分时间以现金形式支出。

不过，拉姆齐承认，一次性投资所有东西在情感上可能会很困难。

“美元平均成本的唯一原因是，如果你在情感上不致力于这一过程，”他说，“当特朗普第一次决定轰炸伊朗并且市场下跌时，你会惊慌失措。”

趋势：已经有超过 150 万人通过 Stash 悄悄进行投资——从 1 美元开始，通过日常购买赚取股票，并通过 Stash+ 获得 3% 的 IRA 匹配。

拉姆齐警告说，投资者应该预见到一段时期的动荡。

“他会做特朗普的事情。你可以指望它，”拉姆齐说。 “我不知道下一个会是什么，但将会有特朗普的事情。有时它会导致市场上涨。有时它会导致市场下跌。”

他补充说，无数其他事件也可以影响市场，从地缘政治冲突到经济新闻和意外的全球事态发展。

在整个谈话中，拉姆齐列举了市场在大幅下跌后复苏的例子。他讨论了 COVID-19 大流行初期的急剧抛售，当时股市因不确定性蔓延到整个经济而暴跌。

“随着时间的推移，市场会恢复常识，”拉姆齐说。

他还提到了最近市场对涉及伊朗的紧张局势的反应，并补充说，一些由头条新闻驱动的最大跌幅的恢复速度往往比投资者预期的要快。

另请参阅：大多数预算应用程序都会忽略您的投资。 Empower 则不然 — 它将您的 401(k)、IRA、银行和信用账户同步到一个实时仪表板中。

拉姆齐传达的更广泛的信息是，长期投资者在经济低迷时期恐慌并抛售时通常会陷入麻烦。 “除了中途下车的人之外，没有人在过山车上受伤，”他说。 “所以，如果你认为你会跳，美元成本平均，因为它可以让你不至于惊慌失措。”

并不是每个人都能得到拉姆齐回答的个人投资问题。对于试图决定如何进行大笔投资、为退休做准备或制定长期财务策略的人们，AdviserMatch 可以将他们与提供投资和财务规划指导的财务顾问联系起来。咨询是无义务的，只需大约两分钟即可开始。

拉姆齐表示，就加布里埃尔的情况而言，如果他能够在不惊慌的情况下应对市场的涨跌，那么立即投入 35 万美元将是最有力的长期数学案例。

阅读下一篇： 储蓄超过 100 万美元的退休人员正在重新考虑他们的税务策略 - 这就是为什么有些人转向专业顾问的原因

不仅仅在市场上创造财富

建立有弹性的投资组合意味着超越单一资产或市场趋势的思维。经济周期发生变化，行业兴衰，没有一项投资在所有环境下都能表现良好。这就是为什么许多投资者希望通过提供房地产、固定收益机会、贵金属甚至自我管理退休账户的平台来实现多元化。通过将风险分散到多个资产类别，可以更轻松地管理风险、获得稳定的回报并创造不只与一家公司或行业的命运挂钩的长期财富。

在杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 的支持下，Arrived Homes 使房地产投资变得更容易，进入门槛较低。投资者只需 100 美元即可购买单户出租屋和度假屋的部分股份。这使得日常投资者能够多元化投资房地产、收取租金收入并建立长期财富，而无需直接管理房产。

住宅房地产历来为投资者提供了收入潜力和长期升值，但直接拥有可能既昂贵又耗时。 ARK7 使投资者能够购买租赁物业的部分股份，无需承担物业管理责任即可获得潜在的租金收入和房地产投资。通过降低进入门槛，该平台为投资者提供了另一种多元化方式
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
